content.full_text:
May 25, 1990

Fair Political Practices
Technical Division
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

ATTN: LEGAL DIVISION
RE: Statement of Economic Interests
Form 721 & 730

Ladies and Gentlemen:

I have a Planning Commissioner with several questions regarding the proper filing of the Statement of Economic Interest, Forms 721 (deadline is May 31, 1990); and Form 730 (June 21, 1990). Commissioner would like written response to his questions and concerns so that proper forms are filed in accordance to law.

BACKGROUND:

Commissioner appointed on May 15; assumed office at first meeting on May 21, 1990 for a one-year term. Commissioner is Attorney At Law (criminal and personal injury) in private practice with an office in the City of Pacific Grove. However, he has on occasion, and no doubt will have in the future, clients filing suit within and outside the jurisdiction (City of Marina) against the Public Safety Department or City of Marina municipality.

QUESTIONS &/OR ISSUE:

1) Must he report income from clients who do not live or own property within the jurisdiction (City of Marina)?

2) Must he report income from criminal clients who live in the jurisdiction?

3) Must he report income from clients he represents in suits against the City of Marina and/or the Marina Public Safety Department?

4) Must he report income from clients who live in or own property within the jurisdiction when settlement claims (income) do not come directly from the City of Marina municipality?

5) In the event a client living in the jurisdiction at the time the claim/suit is filed, moves out of the jurisdiction, and the settlement payment/income is received from client who no longer resides in jurisdiction must commissioner report such income?

6) Must he report "NET" or "GROSS" income from settlements received from the operations of his business in Pacific Grove?

Your assistance in responding to these concerns at your earliest convenience would be greatly appreciated.

Thanking you in advance for your attention and assistance.

Sincerely,

JOY P. JUN SAY
City Clerk

cc: City Manager John Longley
    Robert T. Hatcher, Planning Commissioner
